summary:
Potassium oxalate combined with sodium fluoride are chosen as anticoagulants because these agents effectively inhibit glycolysis while preventing coagulation; this ensures stable glucose readings during testing procedures without altering baseline values significantly or introducing errors due to post-collection changes such as hemolysis leading to falsely elevated results from increased lactose production via anaerobic metabolism pathways initiated after red cell lyses occur naturally if no proper preservative measures were taken beforehand thus compromising accuracy entirely thereby necessitating strict adherence towards recommended practices outlined herein ensuring reliable outcomes consistently across multiple studies conducted under similar conditions highlighting their efficacy particularly when dealing directly related issues concerning hyperglycemia management strategies employed clinically today especially those requiring frequent monitoring like diabetic patients who may require regular assessments using standardized methods adhering strictly per guidelines set forth by professional bodies including but not limited to ADA AACE etc... To summarize succinctly focusing only upon core elements pertinent solely toward addressing immediate query posed initially :

Potassium Oxalate + Sodium Fluoride serve best among available options since they provide necessary stability against enzymatic degradation preserving true concentration intact facilitating precise measurement essential for diagnostic purposes accurately reflecting patient’s current metabolic state crucial info needed promptly often life-saving decisions hinging heavily off correct interpretation obtained quickly reliably minimizing potential risks associated inaccurate lab reports potentially misleading clinicians treating complex cases demanding high degree precision vigilance accordingly efficiently optimizing overall care delivery system enhancing quality standards upheld universally recognized benchmarks established reputable organizations involved healthcare field
